codeReview.summary: Commit 83c9d237255cc46b5b665102e286613e0f6e012f adds the focused schemaless foreign bootstrap regression and the current runtime code satisfies the feature contract: explicit-id foreign persistence routes missing-table/not-found child failures through the same Store-wide ensure path used for root saves, so a fresh schemaless parent save auto-creates both parent and child tables without any foreign-only table-definition exception.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library guidance captures the new Store-wide schemaless bootstrap rule, but it still does not record the separately-proven schema-managed vs schemaless boundary that was added immediately afterward. Workers reviewing save/bootstrap behavior must infer from README/runtime docs and the later schema_managed_runtime_applies_registered_inventory regression that managed-open schema application is a distinct contract from schemaless first-save bootstrap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md and .factory/library/nested-store.md mention schemaless bootstrap as a Store-wide rule, but neither mentions the later contract split proven by commit 2d714404380b067da999c65a8ae45cd85ece07a9 (README.md, core/src/connection/mod.rs, core/tests/integration_db.rs:1673).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission artifacts for this feature are incomplete: the feature is marked completed with worker session 8a552dd5-f5e9-4310-859a-cbe2ecd9e3f2, but there is no matching handoff file.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature definition, scrutiny-validator transcript skeleton evidence, the landed regression commit 83c9d237255cc46b5b665102e286613e0f6e012f, the relevant runtime persistence seam in core/src/lib.rs, the focused tests in core/tests/integration_db.rs, and the mission shared-state files. The feature contract is satisfied: schemaless graph saves bootstrap Store-backed foreign child tables through the same Store-wide persistence behavior already used for root models, preserve RecordId storage at rest, and hydrate correctly on follow-up reads. Mission metadata remains incomplete because the recorded worker session has no handoff artifact, but that did not reveal a code defect.
